meta.analyst_notes: Sales demo recorded via Google Meet (fuz-jgjm-sdl). Presenter: BTC Transfer (CRM Mate seller). Prospect: Nikita T. Language: Russian. Transcript processed by Whisper small model (noisy). All 173 unique frames reviewed. Whisper noise corrections applied: серымка/сиремка=CRM Mate, китор/кейтор=Keitaro, байнг=buying/media buying, нафор/нетвор=network, байнра=buyer, паладеум=Paladium, хайтклик=HideClick.

product.name: CRM Mate
product.confirmed_from: UI logo visible in all product frames

vendor.company_name: CRM Mate (startup, no formal brand name stated)
vendor.team_size: ~7 people: 3 co-owners, 3 programmers, 1 support/integrator who also codes
vendor.development_model: Feature-driven, reactive (features added on client request); no formal roadmap or release calendar
vendor.update_delivery: Rolling, non-uniform updates per client. Tested on early adopters, then rolled out to others. Changelog communicated via chat.
vendor.security: External penetration testing performed. 2FA via Google Authenticator required to export data.
vendor.support: Integrator available near 24/7; integration SLA ~15 minutes average

pricing.included: All features, support, and integrations included in single price
pricing.notes: Stated explicitly at end of demo: '700 dollars a month, one fixed tariff, includes support and CRM itself'

prospect_context.primary_use_case: Media buying (Facebook) as primary, Google as secondary from month 2-3
prospect_context.decision_timeline: 1 week (until next Monday); other demos scheduled

dashboard.description: Overview page with key KPI widgets
widgets: Caps widget (shows cap fill status per campaign)
dashboard.notes: Presenter describes it as 'eye candy' - detailed analytics available in other sections

lead_distribution.description: Core routing engine that assigns incoming leads to campaigns
routing_in_development: User (buyer) - visible in test version but not publicly announced
lead_distribution.distribution_logic: Weight-based proportional distribution; Daily Cap determines lead volume; Weight multiplier adjusts priority ratio between campaigns
lead_distribution.weight_cap_explanation: If campaign A has daily cap 100 and weight 1, campaign B has daily cap 50 and weight 1 - leads are distributed at 2:1 ratio matching cap sizes. Changing weight to 2 on campaign A doubles its intake rate.
lead_distribution.hold_mechanism: Leads that fail routing go to Hold status; can be re-sent manually or automatically via Cold Overflows

general_tab_fields.Name: Campaign name
general_tab_fields.Sources: Allowed sources (multi-select, e.g. Google, FB, Instagram, TikTok, TestWeb, web2)
general_tab_fields.Lang: Language filter (By Leads = auto-detect from lead)
general_tab_fields.Country Group: Use predefined country group instead of individual countries
general_tab_fields.Campaign Statuses: Toggle to enable custom status mapping

caps.daily_cap: Maximum leads per day per campaign

time_cap.granularity: Per day of week (Mon-Sun) with start/end time (HH:MM:SS)
time_cap.apply_to_all: Button to copy one day's schedule to all days

caps.source_caps_in_development: Planned: per-source daily cap within campaign (e.g. Google: 20, FB: 30 from total cap 50)
caps.cap_stats_module: Separate 'Cap' view under Statistics showing cap fill status across all campaigns
caps.telegram_cap_alerts: Bot can send hourly cap fill status notifications

campaign_groups.description: Priority-based grouping of campaigns for waterfall distribution
campaign_groups.purpose: Define strict priority order: leads try highest-priority group first, cascade down on rejection/cap fill
group_fields: Campaigns list with: Name, Time Cap, Priority (integer), Cap used/total, Weight, Only Duplicates flag
campaign_groups.priority_logic: Lower priority number = higher priority. Priority 0 = equal distribution among group members
campaign_groups.only_duplicates_flag: Mark campaign as 'only accept leads that already bounced from other campaigns'
campaign_groups.cpa_only_offer_flag: Same as Only Duplicates - for CPA campaigns that accept rejects
campaign_groups.use_case_example: Main brokers in group priority 1; backup brokers in priority 2; CPA duplicate buyers in priority 3 (last resort)

delayed_actions.description: Schedule future changes to campaign settings
delayed_actions.purpose: Avoid making live changes during traffic pouring hours; pre-configure tomorrow's campaign settings tonight at a scheduled time
delayed_actions.planned_additions: Daily cap changes and weight changes via delayed actions (in development)
delayed_actions.history: Completed actions log shown on same tab
delayed_actions.example_use_case: At 19:00 traffic stops; pre-configure 19:05 action to swap funnels for next day's pour

cold_overflows_injector.description: Automated re-injection engine for held/unsent leads
cold_overflows_injector.function: Automatically resend leads from Hold/unsent pool to campaigns on a schedule; enables second-chance distribution
cold_overflows_injector.use_case: Leads that fell into Hold due to cap or no campaign match are retried automatically at intervals

functions: Send single lead by distribution (routes through normal campaign selection logic)
Send single lead to specific campaign (manual override)

leads_module.search: By Email, ID, or Phone (top search bar)
leads_module.date_filter: Send Date filter with period selector (This Year, etc.)

tabs.Send/Hold: Active leads in queue or hold (shown as combined count, e.g. 102)
tabs.Invalid: Rejected as invalid (e.g. 3-52 depending on filter)

fields: Mass Search (Phone/Email/IP/Name) - textarea for bulk paste
Mass Search (ID/External ID) - textarea for bulk paste
mass_filters.result: Filters lead list to matching records across all tabs
mass_filters.export: Export button available on Mass Filters tab

leads_module.customizable_columns: Users can configure which columns appear via settings modal (Main Fields + UTM Fields sections)
leads_module.status_history_inline: Status history shown inline in lead list with timestamps
leads_module.email_copy: Click email column header to bulk-copy all emails on page (shows 'Emails copied' toast)
leads_module.lead_detail_lead_response: Shows Broker Response (timestamp + raw response e.g. 'Offers not found') and Lead Request JSON payload sent to broker
leads_module.lead_detail_logs: Full action log for this lead
leads_module.lead_detail_anti_fraud: Anti-fraud data: Redirect URLs, Real User Agent, Device, Provider, Browser, Platform, Device Languages, RAM, Connection, GPU, Screen Resolution, Fingerprint, FBToken, UTM params, Action

leads_import.access: Leads Import menu item under Leads
leads_import.function: Upload spreadsheet, map columns, import leads into system

google_table_integration.description: Google Sheets integration for brokers without CRM systems
google_table_integration.access: Google Table menu item under Leads
google_table_integration.function: Broker updates lead statuses in Google Sheets; CRM reads status changes automatically

deposits_approve.access: Deposits Approve menu item under Leads

anti_fraud.description: Multi-layer fraud detection and filtering system

status_fraud_marker.description: Automatic fraud flag triggered when status history shows suspicious pattern (e.g. lead marked valid but then changed to invalid/trash status)
status_fraud_marker.purpose: Anti-shave detection - catches brokers who initially approve leads then retroactively reject them
status_fraud_marker.indicator: Circle/warning icon next to status badge; lead appears in Fraud tab filter
status_fraud_marker.example: Lead goes New -> No send -> New -> Wrong age -> Call back -> Wrong Info; 'Wrong Info' triggers fraud marker

fraud_filter_tab.description: Dedicated fraud filter in leads section

blacklists.server_ips: Block leads originating from known server/datacenter IP ranges (detects automated bots)

anti_fraud.ip_tracking: Tracks originating IP, detects if lead came from a server/VPN vs. real residential IP
anti_fraud.fingerprinting: Browser fingerprint captured (GPU, RAM, screen resolution, device languages, connection type)

autologin.description: Automated lead registration on broker platforms using real Android devices
autologin.technology: Real physical Android smartphones (not emulators); real residential proxies
autologin.fingerprints: Real device fingerprints from actual phones (not spoofed)
autologin.purpose: Register leads on broker platforms to increase FTD conversion; circumvent broker bot detection
autologin.autologin_id_field: Each autologin attempt logged with Autologin ID visible in lead columns
autologin.generate_auto_toggle: Toggle on New Lead form to queue lead for autologin processing
autologin.notes: Presented as key differentiator for media buying clients; especially valuable for Google traffic

status_management.description: Flexible status and status group configuration

status_groups.Initial Statuses: Statuses that count as 'initial contact' (e.g. New, High potential, Low potential, No answer, Duplicate Broker, Wrong age, No send)
status_groups.Invalid Statuses: Statuses that mark lead as invalid (e.g. Wrong geo, Wrong age, WrongInfo, Wrongnumber, Wrong Info)
status_groups.BlackListed Countries: Countries whose leads are auto-blacklisted (used for blacklist percentage deals)

status_management.web_status: Separate 'Web Status' field - status visible to broker/external party; can differ from internal CRM status

blacklist_percentage_feature.description: Support for deals where broker accepts X% from blacklisted geos
blacklist_percentage_feature.example: RU network: 50% Ukrainian leads paid, 50% go to replacement. Blacklist Countries group + percentage configured per campaign.

buying_view.description: Statistics oriented for media buyers (buying team)

network_view.notes: Separate tab to switch context between buying team view and network team view

statistics.finances_statistics: Financial reconciliation view; can close reconciliation periods

utm_statistics.custom_utm_params: System supports creating new custom UTM parameters as additional columns

finance_module.description: Buyer spend tracking and CPL/ROI calculation
finance_module.cpl_calculation: CRM calculates CPL automatically from spend data + lead counts per funnel/user/period
finance_module.buyer_payment_visibility: Per-campaign payment amount visible only to that buyer (can be set to show lower price than actual sale price to manage margin)
finance_module.blacklist_percentage_accounting: Finance can account for blacklist % deals in revenue calculations

tracker.description: Built-in lightweight traffic tracker (primarily for Google traffic)
tracker.purpose: Alternative to Keitaro for Google buyers; avoids detection of external tracker architecture by Google
tracker.capabilities: Domain management, link creation, basic click/lead tracking
tracker.limitations: Not a full-featured tracker like Keitaro or Binom; sufficient for basic needs; Google cloaking less mature than Facebook cloaking
tracker.roadmap: Planned to evolve into full tracker; may become separate product but included free for CRM Mate users
tracker.postback: Keitaro postback integration supported for external tracker users

domains.auto_cloudflare: CRM can automatically add domain to Cloudflare and configure DNS records pointing to server

gitlab_integration.description: Landings hosted on GitLab first, then deployed to servers from there
benefits: Avoids Google detecting Keitaro/Binom tracker infrastructure in landing server headers
Fast server migration: if server banned, all landings auto-migrate to backup server
Caution: auto-migration can expose pattern to hosting provider leading to cascading bans

servers_module.function: Add server credentials; CRM auto-installs required panel (hosting control panel)
servers_module.use_case: Centralized server management without leaving CRM interface

integrations.setup_flow: 1. Share integration credentials with CRM Mate integrator via chat. 2. Integrator creates integration (~15 min). 3. Test email provided; forward to broker. 4. Broker sends test status/deposit. 5. Done.

services_module.description: Third-party service connections configured within CRM

funnel_groups.description: Group multiple funnels under one alias
example_groups: Tesla (contains: Bitcoin Profit, Cryptex Canon, Belka)
funnel_groups.purpose: Reference a group of funnels in campaign settings instead of selecting each individually

funnel_name_mapping.description: Substitute funnel names when sending to broker
funnel_name_mapping.global_mapping: Settings > Funnels tab: map internal funnel name to broker-facing name globally
funnel_name_mapping.per_campaign_mapping: Campaign > Funnels tab: override funnel name substitution for this specific campaign
funnel_name_mapping.use_case: Keep detailed internal funnel names (Tesla Quiz, Tesla Liteform) while sending unified name (Tesla) to broker

sources_module.notes: Sources used as routing parameter in campaigns and for statistics grouping

country_groups.description: Named groups of countries for reuse in campaign routing
country_groups.purpose: Pre-define geo bundles (e.g. Tier1, DACH, Nordics) and reference by group name in campaigns

users_module.server_ips_column: Associate specific server IPs with user account (fraud/security control)
users_module.teams: Sub-menu item 'Teams' under Users for team grouping

security.two_factor_auth: Google 2FA required to export lead data (enforced system-wide)
security.login_notifications: Popup notification on each new login showing: User, Date, IP, Device, Browser details

activity_log.description: Full audit trail of all user actions
example_entries: Admin412341 updated lead Test Test - Changed fields: status_id 32->23, buy 0->0, sale 0->1, is_fnd_status 0->1, status_type_id 1->2, web_status_id 9->32

tasks_module.notes: Built-in project management for team coordination

notifications.in_app: Bell icon with notification feed; new login alerts show full session details

telegram_bot.configuration: Configurable per user/role what notifications to receive

brokers_module.notes: Manage broker profiles separate from campaign configurations

cloaking.facebook_effectiveness: Built-in cloaking sufficient for Facebook according to presenter
cloaking.google_effectiveness: Built-in cloaking less reliable for Google; Keitaro or separate server architecture recommended for Google

postback.keitaro_postback: Supported; allows external Keitaro installation to send conversion data back to CRM

api.broker_integration_api: REST API for broker integrations (lead submission + status postback)
api.test_lead_flow: Test email endpoint for verifying integration before go-live

data_portability.lead_export: Export button on Leads page (requires 2FA to be enabled)

multi_language.ui_languages: Multiple UI language support (at least Russian and English observed)
multi_language.lead_lang_field: Lead language field; 'By Leads' auto-detection mode

technical_features.responsive: Web application; no mention of mobile app

stated_competitors: Trackbox (mentioned by presenter as known competitor)
key_differentiators_claimed: All-inclusive pricing: one flat fee, no add-on modules
Built-in domain + server management (no need for separate registrar dashboards)
GitLab-hosted landings for fast server migration and reduced Google detection footprint
Native autologin with real Android devices (not emulators) - unique among comparable tools
Anti-shave detection via status history analysis
Delayed Actions for pre-scheduling campaign changes
Campaign Groups with waterfall priority logic
Blacklist percentage accounting for RU/UA mixed geo deals
Buying + Network statistics split in single platform
Feature development on client request with ~1 month turnaround
known_weaknesses_admitted: Built-in tracker not yet Keitaro-level for Google traffic
Google cloaking less reliable than Facebook cloaking
No formal roadmap or release calendar
Update rollout is non-uniform across clients
Small team (~7 people) may cause delays on complex features
Development backlog growing as features become more complex
competitive_positioning.target_audience: Crypto/forex affiliate networks and media buying teams (both buying-side and network-side)
competitive_positioning.pricing_vs_competitors: Flat $700/month vs. per-module or per-lead pricing models of competitors like iRev
competitive_positioning.maturity: Early-stage startup with paying clients; actively adding features based on client feedback

public_presence.notes: Primary presence via Telegram; public site crm-mate.com with EN and RU versions

pricing.discrepancy_note: IMPORTANT: Website shows two plans (Solo $200 + Team $700). In demo presenter cited only $700 flat 'one fixed tariff'. Solo plan was NOT mentioned in demo with Nikita T (team buyer) - presenter may have deliberately omitted it, or Solo lacks Android autologin feature.

key_features: Cloaking integrated into CRM (built-in, v2.0 first)
UTM Tag Statistics for campaign analysis
Task Manager for team assignment and expense tracking
Interface improvements and multilingual translations
Tip system for lead management and fraud detection
Auto-disable offers after 5 lead errors
Customizable widgets in profile settings
Test lead generation with realistic IP addresses
Flexible offer working hours configuration
Funnel name customization for leads
Tomorrow tab for capacity planning
Telegram bot notifications for lead metrics
Automatic email generation
Campaign-specific lead routing via camp_id
CPL traffic shaving protection with status notifications
Enhanced fraud analysis using User-Agent and IP data
Status blocking to preserve financial records
Webhook support for lead creation events
Bulk lead injection improvements
Admin function delegation

development_velocity.release_frequency: ~2 major releases/year; reactive feature additions per client request between releases
development_velocity.roadmap_focus: No public roadmap; features from demo in pipeline: source-level caps, expanded tracker, delayed actions for caps

market_sentiment.notes: Zero presence on G2, Capterra, Product Hunt - reputation built entirely via demos and referrals

key_value_props_from_site: Prevents lead loss up to 15% vs. working without CRM
No technical specialists or own servers required
Average advertiser integration time: 5 min (site claim) vs 15 min (demo) - marketing inflation suspected
Buy domain, install SSL, link to server - all inside CRM
Manages Namecheap, Cloudflare, Keitaro, Facebook Conversion API from one interface

referral_program.mechanism: Personal referral link via Telegram bot
referral_program.significance: Active growth mechanism through word-of-mouth in crypto-affiliate community; earnings calculator by plan tier on site

marketing.go_to_market: Demo-led sales (no self-serve demo available), Telegram channel, word-of-mouth, referral program

tech_stack: Real physical Android devices for autologin (not emulators) - key differentiator not mentioned on public site
GitLab for landing page hosting and fast server migration
Namecheap / Cloudflare / Dynadot domain management integrations
Built-in cloaking since v2.0 + support for MagickChecker / HideClick / Paladium
Webhook support for lead creation events

compliance_certifications: External penetration test (claimed in demo, unverified)
2FA via Google Authenticator (mandatory for data export)

Solo plan $200 as entry funnel with upsell to Team $700

contradictions_with_meeting.in_demo: Presenter stated one plan: $700/month flat, 'one fixed tariff, includes support and CRM itself'
contradictions_with_meeting.on_website: Website shows two plans: Solo $200/month and Team $700/month
contradictions_with_meeting.implication: Solo plan deliberately omitted for team client, or Solo lacks key features (Android autologin?). Suggests actual entry price is $200 - competitive with lower-end tools.

contradictions_with_meeting.on_website: Site claims: 'average advertiser integration time of 5 minutes'
contradictions_with_meeting.implication: Marketing inflation on website; demo figure (15 min) more credible

data_gaps: Founder / owner names not publicly disclosed
Actual traffic to crm-mate.com (SimilarWeb/SEMrush not checked)
Feature differences between Solo vs Team plan not published
Identity of 'BTC Transfer' presenter pseudonym unknown
web_enrichment.analyst_summary: CRM Mate is an early-stage startup with a real public site (crm-mate.com, EN+RU), 3 documented releases (v1.03-v2.0, 2024-2025), and an active 10%/12-month referral program via Telegram. KEY FINDING: website shows two pricing tiers - Solo $200/month and Team $700/month - while the demo presenter cited only $700 flat. The $200 Solo plan lowers the true entry price significantly and suggests a funnel strategy. Core competitive differentiator (real Android devices for autologin) is absent from the public website - kept as a private sales weapon. Zero public reviews indicate reputation is built entirely through demos and referrals within the Russian-speaking crypto-affiliate community. Trackbox (stated competitor) has enterprise positioning with undisclosed pricing. Main risks: tiny team, no roadmap, reactive development, and the autologin feature is hardware-dependent (scaling physical Android fleet is non-trivial).
